title: autobench.worker v1
description: Worker-agent call event — emitted once per benchmark case when MiniMaxWorker (or any compatible worker) generates code from a prompt. Closes the no-op generate_fn gap so downstream consumers can attribute token cost and latency to the code-generation step (distinct from autobench.improver.v1, which scopes the harness-mutation step). Source: /autobench.

case_id.description: Benchmark case identifier (may be empty for ad-hoc calls)

model.description: Model that produced the code (e.g. MiniMax-M2.7, or a 'primary+fallback (both failed)' string)

prompt_tokens.description: Prompt-token count from the successful HTTP response

completion_tokens.description: Completion-token count from the successful HTTP response

cost_usd.description: Estimated USD cost using MiniMax public pricing

latency_ms.description: Wall-clock latency from generate() start to return

code_preview.description: First 512 characters of the extracted code (markdown fences already stripped)

error.description: Set only when both primary and fallback model failed; describes the cause
